entities.name: AUTOPHAGY
entities.description: A major intracellular trafficking pathway in eukaryotes involved in vacuolar degradation of cytoplasmic constituents, mis-folded proteins, and defective organelles

entities.name: EUKARYOTES
entities.description: Organisms in which autophagy serves as a major intracellular trafficking pathway

entities.name: VACUOLAR DEGRADATION
entities.description: The process of breaking down cytoplasmic constituents, mis-folded proteins, and defective organelles within vacuoles

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC CONSTITUENTS
entities.description: Components of the cytoplasm that are subject to vacuolar degradation

entities.name: MIS-FOLDED PROTEINS
entities.description: Proteins that have failed to fold correctly and are targeted for degradation

entities.name: DEFECTIVE ORGANELLES
entities.description: Damaged cellular organelles targeted for degradation via autophagy

entities.name: OPTIMUM CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental states where autophagy operates at a basal level to maintain homeostasis

entities.name: CELLULAR HOMEOSTASIS
entities.description: The state of steady internal cellular conditions maintained by basal autophagy

entities.name: STRESSED CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental states that induce increased autophagy to provide stress relief

entities.name: STRESS RELIEF
entities.description: The temporal relief from stress provided by the induction of autophagy

entities.name: PLANTS
entities.description: Organisms in which autophagy plays specific roles in development and improvement

entities.name: CROPS
entities.description: Agricultural plants targeted for improvement in stress tolerance, yield, and metabolite production via autophagy

entities.name: STRESS-TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to withstand environmental stress, potentially improved by autophagy

entities.name: DISEASE-TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to withstand disease, potentially improved by autophagy

entities.name: YIELD POTENTIAL
entities.description: The capacity for crop production, which can be enhanced through the application of autophagy

entities.name: METABOLITES OF HIGH ECONOMIC VALUE
entities.description: High-value biological substances whose production can be improved via autophagy

entities.name: BIOTECHNOLOGICAL APPLICATION
entities.description: Strategies to develop modules for applying autophagy to enhance agriculture

entities.name: BIOECONOMY
entities.description: An economic system based on the sustainable use of biological resources, enhanced by autophagy applications

entities.name: SUSTAINABILITY OF AGRICULTURE
entities.description: The long-term viability of agricultural practices, enhanced by the biotechnological application of autophagy
